info.name: BookShare
info.description: BookShare REST + GraphQL test collection. Grows story by story. Run with `npx newman run postman/BookShare.postman_collection.json` for CI-style verification.

item.name: Auth
item.description: Signup + login. Each request runs in order; the signup happy-path saves the test email/password so later requests can reuse them.

item.name: Home
item.description: GET /api/v1/home/recent-books — public, returns books with owner.display_name (no contact fields). GraphQL recentBooks reuses the Book.owner cross-service join.

exec: pm.test('owner.email is null for anonymous viewer', () => pm.expect(b.owner.email).to.be.null);

item.name: Profile
item.description: Authenticated profile read + update via /api/v1/profile. Depends on the Auth folder running first so {{token}} is set.

exec: pm.test('display_name was updated', () => pm.expect(body.display_name).to.eql('Test User Renamed'));

item.name: Users
item.description: Public user profile reads at /api/v1/users/:id. Contact fields are returned only when the caller presents a valid JWT.

item.name: Books — Add
item.description: POST /api/v1/books (multipart) and GraphQL addBook. Depends on token captured by the Auth folder's signup test.

exec: pm.test('returns book.author', () => pm.expect(body.book.author).to.eql('J. R. R. Tolkien'));

body.raw: {"query": "mutation Add($input: AddBookInput!) { addBook(input: $input) { id title author year_published cover_object_key status owner_id } }", "variables": {"input": {"title": "Dune", "author": "Frank Herbert", "year_published": 1965, "cover_base64": "__COVER_B64__", "cover_content_type": "image/jpeg"}}}

exec: // Postman doesn't read files from disk in pre-request, so use a baked-in tiny base64 jpeg.

item.name: Books — Browse
item.description: GET /api/v1/books — paginated public catalog. Runs after Books — Add so the catalog is non-empty.

exec: pm.test('first edge has null owner.email', () => pm.expect(e.owner.email).to.be.null);

item.name: Search
item.description: GET /api/v1/search?q=... (public) — title/author + owner-name search. Includes the Book.my_active_reservation null stub.

exec: pm.test('at least one book matched by owner', () => pm.expect(ownerMatches.length).to.be.at.least(1));
pm.test('owner.email is null for anonymous', () => pm.expect(b.owner.email).to.be.null);

item.name: Covers
item.description: Public GET /api/v1/covers/:object_key — proxies MinIO through the gateway. Depends on createdCoverKey captured by the Books — Add happy path.

exec: pm.test('cache-control set to one day public', () => {

item.name: Books — List
item.description: GET /api/v1/users/:id/books REST + GraphQL user(id) { books }. Depends on Auth folder for {{userId}}/{{token}} and Books — Add for the previously created book.

item.name: GET /api/v1/users/:id/books — own user (200 with the book added)
POST /auth/signup — second user (sets up empty-user test)

body.raw: {"query": "query U($id:ID!){user(id:$id){id display_name books{id title author year_published cover_object_key status}}}", "variables": {"id": "{{userId}}"}}

item.name: Books — Edit
item.description: PUT /api/v1/books/:id (owner-only) + public GET /api/v1/books/:id + GraphQL book(id) cross-service join + editBook mutation.

exec: pm.test('updated_at is later than created_at', () => pm.expect(body.book.updated_at >= body.book.created_at).to.be.true);

item.name: POST /auth/signup — third user (sets up non-owner test)
query book(id) { owner } — anonymous (contact fields null)

body.raw: {"query": "query B($id:ID!){book(id:$id){id title owner{id display_name email phone address}}}", "variables": {"id": "{{createdBookId}}"}}

item.name: query book(id) { owner } — authenticated (contact fields populated)

body.raw: {"query": "query B($id:ID!){book(id:$id){id title owner{id display_name email phone address}}}", "variables": {"id": "{{createdBookId}}"}}

item.name: Books — Replace cover
item.description: PUT /api/v1/books/:id/cover — owner-only multipart upload. Rotates cover_object_key.

item.name: Books — Delete
item.description: DELETE /api/v1/books/:id (owner-only) + GraphQL deleteBook. WARNING: the happy-path test permanently removes {{createdBookId}}; folder must run BEFORE any further test referencing that variable.

item.name: JWT middleware
item.description: Smoke-tests that the gateway gates protected /api/v1/* paths after enabling JWT verification in story 1.3.

exec: pm.test('status is 404 — auth passed, route just does not exist yet', () => pm.response.to.have.status(404));

item.name: GraphQL
item.description: Signup and login via the /graphql endpoint.

exec: pm.test('status is 401 (Apollo respects http.status extension)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(401));
